title: Is it okay
wikitext:
[]: Is it okay...?             Quote Post by dylkimaloc » Wed Feb 17, 2010 2:32 am Is it okay for me to not really understand much of this show? I keep getting half way through it and it doesn't do anything for me and yet I keep getting drawn back and start watching it again and repeat. And after watching it, I still don't understand much of what it's trying to say? I know that NGE is complex but I am a complex thinker myself and still can't crack this show. So basically, is it okay for me not to understand much of what this show is trying to say? I'm new to all this stuff too, so please don't get upset or frustrated with me.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Feb 17, 2010 2:43 am I don't think any of us gets it all, and we certainly find plenty to disagree about. Just lurk a bit, ask questions, and have a good time while you do it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 17, 2010 3:17 am Eva is like a mirror -- a lot of what you see in it is how it reflects your own condition. In that respect, it really is as complex as you make it.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Feb 17, 2010 6:35 am Simple answer - Yes, it's nothing major.

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Wed Feb 17, 2010 7:20 am It depends on WHAT you are trying to understand.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Wed Feb 17, 2010 8:27 am Old Administrator Proverb: ObsessiveMathsFreaks wrote: We're all adrift on the stormy seas of Evangelion, desperately trying to gather what flotsam can be snatched from the gale into a somewhat seaworthy interpretation so that we can at last reach the shores of reason and respite.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Feb 17, 2010 8:45 am I've been on these forums for over three years and I don't know a thing yet.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Feb 17, 2010 2:37 pm I guess not everyone receives enlightenment upon first viewing. Keep watching, my child, and when you least expect it understanding will come. Besides, all we have to do is get Ornette to email Anno when we want to know something. They're acquainted IRL.

[]:             Quote Post by SleepS » Wed Feb 17, 2010 3:07 pm The trap is trying to find the right answer as opposed to your answer. Anno wants us to interpret the series in our own ways. I think that's important to keep in mind while debating theories and such here.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Wed Feb 17, 2010 4:43 pm I think we all come to our own conclusion on how the series ends. for Example: Some think episode 25 and 26 run inside Shinji's mind in EoE, And some people think episode 25 and 26 just didn't happen at all. It's basically up to you.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Wed Feb 17, 2010 4:48 pm Strange, I seem to understand the series just fine, although I had to see EoE a second time to understand what happened once Rei Lilith appeared and all.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 17, 2010 4:52 pm SleepS wrote: The trap is trying to find the right answer as opposed to your answer.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 17, 2010 4:56 pm If you just look at the superfices, the story spells itself out using enough appropriate genre tropes to be quite plain and simple. It's a story about multiple notionally allied factions competing to launch a Singularity bootstrapped on alien technology found under Hakone in Japan and in the Antarctic; and the hard take-off of that Singularity. Where things may be a little confused is that EoTV is a Friendly Upload scenario with a GOOD END; whereas EoE is a non-Friendly SysOp scenario, which concludes with the wrecking of the Earth when the SysOp heeds a user command to crash and burn the system, and a final BAD END.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 17, 2010 9:32 pm Ojisan, stop trying to confuse/brainwash the newcomer!

[]:             Quote Post by planet news » Wed Feb 17, 2010 9:54 pm Mr. Tines wrote: It's a story about multiple notionally allied factions ... burn the system, and a final BAD END.

[]:             Quote Post by dylkimaloc » Wed Feb 17, 2010 10:29 pm How about when it comes to Rebuild? I seem to be more accepting to it than the series. So is it okay for me to accept Rebuild as to what's happening in the Eva world?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 17, 2010 10:38 pm dylkimaloc wrote: How about when it comes to Rebuild? I seem to be more accepting to it than the series. So is it okay for me to accept Rebuild as to what's happening in the Eva world?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Feb 17, 2010 10:47 pm dylkimaloc wrote: So is it okay for me to accept Rebuild as to what's happening in the Eva world?

[]:             Quote Post by dylkimaloc » Wed Feb 17, 2010 10:49 pm NemZ wrote: dylkimaloc wrote: So is it okay for me to accept Rebuild as to what's happening in the Eva world?

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Wed Feb 17, 2010 11:06 pm It's my nature...sorry
